skill.name: quadas-c-assessment-for-diagnostic-accuracy-studies
skill.description: Automated bias assessment for diagnostic accuracy studies using QUADAS-C criteria. Requires full text input.
skill.summary: Automates QUADAS-C risk-of-bias assessment for diagnostic accuracy studies comparing multiple index tests.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 46 potential issues, but manual code review confirmed all are FALSE POSITIVES. This is a legitimate medical research skill for QUADAS-C bias assessment with Python scripts for PDF text extraction and structured evaluation. Detected patterns (C2 keywords, weak crypto algorithms, backtick execution) resolve to medical domain identifiers, Markdown code formatting, and harmless class names or descriptions.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' in 8 locations. All are false positives: the matches are on argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter class name, YAML front-matter descriptions, and ordinary prose text. No cryptographic code exists in this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern matching on harmless strings like 'RawDescriptionHelpFormatter' and 'description' fields. No cryptographic imports, operations, or algorithms present in any file.

low_findings.title: C2 Keywords (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'C2 keywords' at lines 31-34 of prompts.md. 'C2' in this context refers to 'Comparison Domain 2' (Index Test domain) of the QUADAS-C medical assessment tool, not Command & Control infrastructure. These are standard medical research signaling questions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is clearly labeled as 'QUADAS-C Prompts' and C2.1-C2.4 are standard QUADAS-C signaling questions for the Index Test domain.

low_findings.title: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged backtick usage in SKILL.md and quadas_c.py as command execution. In SKILL.md, backticks are Markdown formatting for inline code and fenced code blocks. In quadas_c.py:6, the backtick is inside a Python comment formatting the function name 'call_llm'. No actual shell execution occurs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All instances are either Markdown code formatting (single backticks for inline code, triple backticks for code blocks) or backtick-enclosed function names in Python comments.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'System reconnaissance' across the audit result JSON, prompts.md, and SKILL.md. All instances are structured evaluation data, medical research questions, or documentation about output discipline. No reconnaissance behavior present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Audit JSON contains structured evaluation scores and assertions. Prompts.md contains medical signaling questions. SKILL.md contains output discipline guidance.

low_findings.title: Python File Write/Append (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged open() with write mode in extract_pdf.py:53. This is the core legitimate function of a PDF text extraction tool -- writing extracted text to an output file chosen by the user. No unauthorized file access or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: File write is the documented purpose of the extract_pdf.py utility. Output path is user-specified or derived from input filename. No system file paths or suspicious destinations.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged GitHub URL in SKILL.md:7. This is a standard Markdown reference link to the skill's source repository. No network requests are made by the skill code itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is a documentation reference to https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills. The Python scripts contain no HTTP client code or network calls.

content.user_title: Assess Diagnostic Accuracy Bias with QUADAS-C
content.value_statement: Researchers conducting systematic reviews need a standardized, reproducible method for evaluating bias in diagnostic accuracy studies that compare multiple index tests. This skill automates the QUADAS-C assessment process by extracting paper text, identifying diagnostic methods, and generating structured risk-of-bias reports across all four assessment domains.
actual_capabilities: Extract full text from PDF research papers using the extract_pdf.py utility
Identify diagnostic methods compared in a given study from paper text
Assess risk of bias across 4 QUADAS-C domains: Patient Selection, Index Test, Reference Standard, and Flow and Timing
Apply QUADAS-C signaling questions and risk rules to determine Low, High, or Unclear ratings
Generate structured JSON reports with domain-level risk ratings and intermediate reasoning
Support command-line input via text file or direct text argument for flexible usage
limitations: Requires full text of the research paper; abstracts or summaries lack sufficient detail for accurate assessment
Only applicable to diagnostic accuracy studies that compare two or more index tests
Assessment accuracy depends on the underlying LLM model quality; the included quadas_c.py provides a mock implementation that requires real API integration
Does not support batch processing of multiple papers in a single run

use_cases.title: Systematic Literature Review Bias Screening
use_cases.description: Screen a collection of diagnostic accuracy studies for risk of bias during a systematic review. Extract text from each paper PDF and produce standardized QUADAS-C assessments.

use_cases.title: Meta-Analysis Preparation
use_cases.description: Prepare bias assessment data for meta-analysis of diagnostic accuracy studies. Generate consistent risk ratings across multiple studies to support quantitative synthesis.

use_cases.title: Clinical Guideline Development
use_cases.description: Evaluate the quality of diagnostic accuracy evidence during clinical practice guideline development. Document bias assessments to support evidence grading decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic PDF Text Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the full text from this PDF research paper so I can analyze it for bias assessment.
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides a PDF file path and needs the text content extracted before assessment.

prompt_templates.title: Single Study QUADAS-C Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a QUADAS-C bias assessment on the following diagnostic accuracy study. Identify the diagnostic methods compared and evaluate each of the four domains: Patient Selection, Index Test, Reference Standard, and Flow and Timing.
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides full paper text and needs a complete bias assessment with domain-level risk ratings.

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Analysis Across Multiple Studies
prompt_templates.prompt: Run QUADAS-C assessments on the following three diagnostic accuracy studies and compare their risk-of-bias profiles across all four domains. Highlight any consistent patterns or notable differences.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to compare bias ratings across multiple studies for systematic review reporting.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Domain Evaluation with Specific Questions
prompt_templates.prompt: Focus the QUADAS-C assessment on only the Flow and Timing domain for this study. Pay special attention to missing data patterns and whether the same reference standard was used for all index tests.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a targeted evaluation of a specific domain with emphasis on particular signaling questions.

output_examples.input: Assess bias for this diagnostic accuracy study comparing MRI and CT for liver cancer detection. The study included 150 patients who all received both scans, with surgical pathology as the reference standard.
output_examples.output:
QUADAS-C Assessment Results:

Domain 1 - Patient Selection: Low Risk
Both index tests used the same patient cohort with clear inclusion criteria.

Domain 2 - Index Test: Low Risk
MRI and CT results were interpreted independently by blinded radiologists.

Domain 3 - Reference Standard: Low Risk
Surgical pathology was applied uniformly as the reference standard for all patients.

Domain 4 - Flow and Timing: Low Risk
Appropriate interval between tests; no missing data reported.

Overall: Low risk of bias across all domains.

output_examples.input: Extract text from the PDF at paper.pdf and then assess bias using QUADAS-C.
output_examples.output:
Step 1: Extracted text from paper.pdf (12 pages, 8,450 words)
Step 2: Detected 3 diagnostic methods - Ultrasound, CT, and MRI
Step 3: QUADAS-C Assessment:
- Patient Selection: Low Risk
- Index Test: High Risk (ultrasound results were interpreted with knowledge of CT findings)
- Reference Standard: Low Risk
- Flow and Timing: Unclear Risk (interval between index tests not reported)

Final: Mixed risk profile - caution needed in Index Test domain.

best_practices: Always verify the quality of extracted PDF text before running bias assessment. Check for garbled characters or missing sections.
Review the intermediate outputs for each domain to understand the reasoning behind risk ratings before accepting results.
When comparing multiple studies, process each study separately and compile results manually to ensure accurate per-study assessments.
anti_patterns: Do not use truncated text or abstracts for assessment. QUADAS-C requires complete study details including methods, results, and limitations sections.
Do not assume the skill can evaluate non-diagnostic studies. QUADAS-C applies only to diagnostic accuracy studies comparing multiple index tests.
Do not use the mock LLM implementation in quadas_c.py for production assessments. Replace call_llm() with a real LLM API integration for actual use.

faq.question: What is QUADAS-C?
faq.answer: QUADAS-C is a tool for assessing risk of bias in diagnostic accuracy studies that compare two or more index tests. It extends the QUADAS-2 tool with additional comparison-specific domains.

faq.question: What types of studies can this skill evaluate?
faq.answer: This skill evaluates diagnostic accuracy studies that compare at least two diagnostic methods or index tests against a reference standard.

faq.question: Do I need to provide the full text of the paper?
faq.answer: Yes. The assessment requires the full paper text. Abstracts do not contain enough detail about study methods and results for accurate bias evaluation.

faq.question: What domains does the QUADAS-C assessment cover?
faq.answer: Four domains: Patient Selection, Index Test, Reference Standard, and Flow and Timing. Each domain receives a risk rating of Low, High, or Unclear.

faq.question: Can I process PDF files directly?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the extract_pdf.py script to extract text from PDF files. Then pass the extracted text to the assessment script for bias evaluation.

faq.question: How are risk ratings determined?
faq.answer: The skill answers a set of signaling questions for each domain and applies QUADAS-C rules. If all questions indicate low concern, the domain gets Low risk. Any concern raises it to High or Unclear.
